description_long: This object consists of a vertical cylindrical pole supporting two large, square, horn-shaped megaphones arranged one above the other. The megaphones are oriented in opposite directions, each with a wide, flared mouth and a narrow, boxy base. Four curved, black sound baffles extend outward from the sides of the megaphones, connected by thin support rods that form a diamond-shaped frame around the horns. The pole appears to be metallic with a brownish finish, while the megaphones and baffles are a combination of matte black and metallic gray. The overall design is robust and utilitarian, suitable for outdoor public address or emergency alert systems in urban environments.
description: A tall, brown metallic pole supports two large, black and gray horn-shaped megaphones with curved sound baffles and a diamond-shaped support frame. The structure is industrial and designed for outdoor public announcements.
description_view_0: Front view showing both megaphones stacked vertically, with the diamond-shaped frame and curved baffles clearly visible.
description_view_1: Side view nearly identical to the front, emphasizing the symmetry and alignment of the megaphones and baffles.
description_view_2: Rear view, mirroring the front, with both megaphones and the supporting frame visible from behind.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again similar to the front, highlighting the uniformity and structure of the megaphone assembly.
attribute_mouthpiece_design: Wide, square, flared horn with a boxy base; no distinct mouthpiece attachment or covering visible.
